title: Unsoundness of init value handling in opt_merge, opt_clean, and/or FfInitVals

features.name: dff_cells_with_init_attribute
features.description: Code should include multiple $dff cells each having an \init attribute specifying initial values for flip-flop outputs
features.snippet:
cell $dff $ff1
  parameter \CLK_POLARITY 1'1
  parameter \WIDTH 1
  connect \D 1'0
  connect \Q $q1 [0]
end
attribute \init 2'00
wire width 2 $q1

features.name: multi_width_dff_with_constant_input
features.description: Code should include a multi-bit width $dff cell (width > 1) with a constant input matching its init value
features.snippet:
cell $dff $ff3
  parameter \CLK_POLARITY 1'1
  parameter \WIDTH 2
  connect \D 2'00
  connect \Q { $q1 [1] $q2 [1] }
end
attribute \init 2'00
wire width 2 $q2

features.name: wire_with_init_attribute
features.description: Code should include wires that have \init attributes specifying their initial reset values, separate from the DFF cells
features.snippet:
attribute \init 2'00
wire width 2 $q1
attribute \init 2'00
wire width 2 $q2

features.name: dff_outputs_merged_via_common_wire
features.description: Code should include multiple DFF cells whose Q outputs are connected to a common wire, creating a fanin/fanout relationship that triggers init value conflict during optimization
features.snippet:
connect \q [0] $q1 [0]
connect \q [1] $q2 [0]
connect \q [2] $q1 [1]
connect \q [3] $q2 [1]
wire output 2 width 4 \q

features.name: private_wires_shared_between_dffs
features.description: Code should include private-named wires ($q1, $q2) that are shared between multiple DFF cells, with some bits used in different contexts across cells
features.snippet:
wire width 2 $q1
wire width 2 $q2
connect \Q $q1 [0]
connect \Q $q2 [0]
connect \Q { $q1 [1] $q2 [1] }
